acceptance_criteria_patches.title: MCP agent ergonomics: oversized responses, boilerplate affordances, metadata-only summaries
acceptance_criteria_patches.proposed_acceptance_criteria: Oversized MCP responses are refused or summarized without flooding agent context: responses above the configured budget return metadata-only summaries, continuation handles, and explicit open/read-next affordances. Boilerplate affordances are removed from normal-size responses. Fixtures prove large result sets stay under budget, continuation handles resolve to the intended evidence, and the user-visible guidance names the next safe action.

acceptance_criteria_patches.title: OriginSpec: one package per origin, dispatch order derived from declared strictness
acceptance_criteria_patches.proposed_acceptance_criteria: OriginSpec is the sole origin-dispatch contract. Each origin declares detector, strictness, parser entry point, raw fixture, normalized fixture, schema mapping, and fidelity/completeness notes. Dispatch order is deterministic and tested against ambiguous fixtures. Adding a new origin without an OriginSpec fails a registry completeness check.

acceptance_criteria_patches.title: Local embedding lane via OpenAI-compatible provider (LiteLLM gateway)
acceptance_criteria_patches.proposed_acceptance_criteria: A local OpenAI-compatible embedding provider works through the same provider abstraction as cloud providers. Context retrieval can opt into the local provider without changing callers. Secrets and provider URLs are not logged. Interface-level fixtures prove local/cloud parity for request shape, error handling, disabled-provider behavior, and retrieval metadata.

acceptance_criteria_patches.title: Materialize hook events + OTLP spans into queryable evidence
acceptance_criteria_patches.proposed_acceptance_criteria: Hook events and OTLP spans materialize into queryable evidence tables with stable object refs, idempotent replay, parser fingerprints, and fixture coverage. Replaying the same input does not duplicate rows. Query surfaces can select the materialized events by session, repo/worktree, time, and evidence tier.

acceptance_criteria_patches.title: Report: polylogue forensics for Hermes sessions

The report includes at least one happy-path fixture, one missing-field/degraded fixture, and one fidelity limitation that renders visibly instead of silently disappearing.

memories_to_add.value: DELIVERY UPGRADE (2026-07-06): Treat Beads as a delivery system, not a linear queue. Active beads carry release/lane/readiness labels, proof artifacts, and hidden blockers. The source topological order remains safe, but implementation proceeds through gated releases: trust floor -> storage/rebuild -> read/evidence contract -> agent coordination -> variants -> lineage -> live performance -> web cockpit -> analytics -> embeddings -> interop -> public legibility -> consolidation -> horizon.

READINESS RULES: ready means no active hard blocker, acceptance criteria present, enough design/code locality to start, no unmodeled product contract, and PR-shaped size. Grade A=implementation-ready, B=local inspection needed, C=acceptance criteria missing, D=horizon-ready, E=spec needed. "No hard blocker" alone is not sufficient.

PUBLIC CLAIMS GATE: README, demos, launch material, analytics, and reports may publish numbers or behavioral claims only after evidence-honesty gates, temporal provenance gates, field-level absence semantics, and citation/object-ref substrate are in place. Unsupported findings must render not_supported/unknown rather than zero or confident prose.

AGENT CONTEXT SAFETY: any agent-authored assertion, preference, variant, advisory, or setup change is a candidate unless judged by the operator/user. Context injection passes through the context scheduler, which emits an inclusion/exclusion ledger with trust class, budget, and evidence refs.

ORIGIN CONTRACT: every importer/exporter declares OriginSpec-style detector, strictness, parser, raw fixture, normalized fixture, parser fingerprint, fidelity/completeness notes, and deterministic ambiguous-dispatch behavior. No new origin enters as a one-off script.
